page: Microsoft Azure E32bds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the EBDSv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, configured with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The memory allocation is 256.0 GB, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. The server includes 1229 GB of local SSD storage and supports Premium Storage for high block storage performance. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates solid mid-range performance across most compute and memory tasks. The server achieved a PassMark CPU score of 36,975.23 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 14,162.0, both placing in the average tier. Memory performance is a notable strength, with the PassMark memory score of 2,815.31 ranking in the top 25% strong tier. Large block memory read bandwidth reaches 151.75 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is 69.57 GB/sec. Image processing benchmarks also show strong performance at 13,629.0.

This configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and storage-heavy applications. It handles Redis SET operations at 31.6 million operations per second and static web serving at 34.04 GB/sec. For machine learning, the server performs well on smaller models, achieving a strong text generation speed of 269.82 tokens/sec on a 135M parameter LLM. However, a clear tradeoff is visible in large-scale AI workloads; prompt processing for a 70B parameter LLM drops to a weak 6.63 tokens/sec, indicating it is not optimized for large-scale LLM inference without dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E32bds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the EBDSv5 family, powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon 8370C processor. It features 32 vCPUs, 256.0 GB of RAM, and 1229 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the server delivers strong memory performance, as evidenced by a PassMark memory score in the top 25% and a large-block read bandwidth of 151.75 GB/sec. Compute benchmarks, including a PassMark CPU score of 36,975.23, generally fall into the average tier. While highly capable for database operations, Redis caching, and small-scale LLM inference, the lack of GPU acceleration results in weak performance for large-scale 70B parameter model processing. This server is designed for memory-intensive enterprise applications, relational databases, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32bds_v5 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, delivering strong memory performance for databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32bds_v5 is a memory-optimized server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1229 GB local storage, ideal for database workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high-capacity local storage and strong memory bandwidth for database and enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 32 vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM
1229 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Strong memory performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
Average-tier compute performance with 36,975.23 PassMark CPU score
Weak performance on large-scale LLM inference workloads
Optimized for databases, Redis caching, and memory-intensive applications
